plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration below the 25% single-name limit.
Sell SMH to lower semiconductor sector exposure.
Add QQQI to increase focus on income generation.
Buy LEU as a new, high-quality long position.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for safety.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce overall leverage.
Trim SPY to meet the 25% single-name concentration limit.
Trim SMH to lower semiconductor sector exposure.
Add to CRWD to increase exposure to a strong AI/Software name.
Buy GUSH as a new, high risk/reward setup.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets approximately 2.5% per month by reducing leverage and concentration while adding to quality positions. It aims for risk-first growth with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Increase exposure to SOXL and add SPAL for leveraged growth.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Add to SMH to increase exposure in a strong sector.
Add to CRWD to increase conviction in a leading tech name.
Buy AVGO as a new, high-potential setup.
Maintain a small cash buffer, focusing on high-risk trades.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by increasing leveraged ETFs and concentrating in high-scoring setups. Expect higher potential gains but also significantly larger drawdowns and leverage decay risks.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (SMH, CRWD).
Trim SPY to reduce concentration.
Significantly increase QQQI for maximum income generation.
Buy XOM as a stable, income-producing asset.
Hold a substantial cash buffer for safety and distributions.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% per month by prioritizing income-generating assets like QQQI and stable names like XOM. It aims for low drawdowns and reliable, though variable, monthly distributions.
